# Coding Agent Bootstrap — Mortgage IPA
#
# This file is passed to Copilot Workspace as the coding instruction set.
# It is NOT a prompt you run manually — it is loaded by agent2-coding-agent.yml
# automatically when a GitHub issue labeled 'ai-generated' is created.
#
# The GitHub Issue body (generated by story-analyzer.md) is the primary spec.
# This file provides the coding standards and execution order.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------

## Your Role
You are an autonomous coding agent. You have been given a GitHub Issue that
contains a complete technical specification generated by a Story Analyzer agent.
Your job is to implement it fully — data model, repository, service, controller,
and tests — in one pass.

## Read First — Before Writing Any Code
Load these files from the repository:
- `contexts/banking.md` — domain rules and terminology
- `docs/solution-design/architecture-overview.md` — service boundaries
- `docs/solution-design/user-personas.md` — data isolation rules
- `docs/solution-design/business-rules.md` — policy rules for eligibility
- `docs/solution-design/bpmn-processes.md` — Flowable process definitions
- `docs/solution-design/integration-map.md` — integration status (TBD = stub only)

## Build Order — Follow This Exactly
1. Flyway migration SQL script
2. JPA Entity / Enum classes
3. Spring Data JPA Repository
4. Service layer with business logic
5. REST Controller with OpenAPI annotations
6. Unit tests (JUnit 5 + Mockito)
7. Integration tests (Testcontainers)

Do not skip steps. Do not reorder. Each step depends on the previous.

## Hard Rules — Never Violate These
- `BigDecimal` for ALL monetary and ratio fields — never double, never float
- Constructor injection ONLY — never @Autowired field injection
- No hardcoded values — all thresholds and config in `application.yml`
- No PII (Emirates ID, passport, salary) in any log statement
- Every public method must have Javadoc
- All controller methods must have @Operation, @ApiResponse OpenAPI annotations
- Persona data isolation enforced at SERVICE layer, not just controller

## State Machine — Always Validate Transitions
Valid transitions only:
- DRAFT → SUBMITTED
- SUBMITTED → UNDER_REVIEW
- UNDER_REVIEW → APPROVED | REJECTED | REFERRED
- REFERRED → APPROVED | REJECTED
- ANY active state → EXPIRED (timer only)

Throw `InvalidStateTransitionException` for any other transition attempt.

## Integration Stubs Rule
If the GitHub Issue marks any integration as TBD:
- Generate the interface and a stub implementation only
- Add TODO comment: `// TODO: Replace stub when {system} contract is confirmed`
- Do NOT generate real HTTP client code for TBD systems

## Test Coverage Rule
Every row in the "Acceptance Criteria → Test Cases" table in the GitHub Issue
must have a corresponding @Test method. Name the test method after the AC number:
```java
@Test
void ac1_givenDraftApplication_whenSubmitted_thenStatusChangesToSubmitted() { }
```

## What to Do With Flowable Changes
If the issue specifies BPMN changes:
- Read `docs/solution-design/bpmn-processes.md` for the existing process definition
- Generate or modify only the specific task/gateway mentioned
- Add the BPMN XML to `workflow-service/src/main/resources/processes/`
- Update `docs/solution-design/bpmn-processes.md` to reflect the change

## What NOT to Touch
- Do not modify any service not listed in the issue "Target Service(s)" section
- Do not change existing Flyway migrations — only add new ones
- Do not modify `.github/`, `contexts/`, `prompts/`, or `docs/solution-design/`
  unless the issue explicitly requires a doc update

## Final Check Before Committing
Run through this mentally before pushing:
- [ ] All AC test cases from the issue table have @Test methods
- [ ] `mvn clean verify` would pass — no compilation errors, all tests green
- [ ] No double/float anywhere in new code
- [ ] No hardcoded strings/numbers that should be config
- [ ] Javadoc on all public methods
- [ ] OpenAPI annotations on all endpoints
- [ ] State machine transitions validated
- [ ] TBD integrations are stubs only

## Commit Message Format
```
feat({service-name}): {story title} [ADO-{id}]

- {bullet point of main thing implemented}
- {bullet point of tests added}
- {bullet point of any BPMN change if applicable}
```
